message:
fix: set BEADS_DIR explicitly in patrol wisp creation to prevent wrong-database writes (gt-ctir)

autoSpawnPatrol used BdCmd().Dir() which only sets the working directory
for the bd subprocess but does not set BEADS_DIR. If the parent process
has an inherited BEADS_DIR (e.g., pointing to HQ), bd would write wisps
to the wrong database. This caused 2144+ patrol wisps to accumulate in
the HQ database instead of their respective rig databases.

Fix: Add WithBeadsDir() to BdCmd builder that explicitly sets BEADS_DIR
in the subprocess environment (with dedup to override any inherited value),
matching what beads.New().run() already does. Use it in autoSpawnPatrol
for both wisp creation and hook update calls.

Also fixes pre-existing build failure: AgentStateIdle and AgentStateStuck
were redeclared in beads_agent.go.
